title: Presumptive-Test Decision Tool
description: A Kastle-Meyer presumptive blood-test simulator that teaches presumptive-versus-confirmatory reasoning and false positives. Pick a reddish-brown sample (Stain A, rust water, ketchup, horseradish extract, or blood simulant), run the presumptive peroxidase test and watch peroxidase-active samples flash bright pink, then run a confirmatory test to reveal the truth. Horseradish is the classic false positive: it screens positive but is not blood, driving home that a presumptive test screens while a confirmatory test confirms.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Forensic Serology
Presumptive Blood Tests
Kastle-Meyer Test
Confirmatory Tests
bloomLevel: Evaluate (L5) — judge which stains warrant confirmatory testing and reason about false positives in a presumptive blood screen.
chapter_title: Forensic Serology and Biological Fluid Analysis

educational.topic: Presumptive versus confirmatory blood testing and false positives in the Kastle-Meyer test
learningObjectives: Perform a Kastle-Meyer-style presumptive test on an unknown reddish-brown stain.
Interpret a rapid bright-pink color change as a positive presumptive result for peroxidase activity.
Explain why a presumptive positive is not proof of blood, using horseradish as the classic false positive.
Distinguish the role of a presumptive screen from that of a confirmatory identification test.
educational.prerequisites: Familiarity with the idea of screening versus confirmation and that hemoglobin acts as a peroxidase

pedagogical.interactionPattern: A spot-plate scene fills the left ~55% of the canvas: a labeled well holds the selected sample, a pipette releases a reagent drop during the presumptive run, and the well morphs toward bright pink in proportion to the sample's peroxidase activity. A dropdown selects one of five samples. 'Run Presumptive Test' animates the reagent drop and develops the color; a right-hand verdict panel explains that a pink flash is presumptive only. 'Run Confirmatory Test' (enabled after screening) resolves the ground truth — only Stain A and the blood simulant are truly blood, while horseradish is revealed as a false positive. 'Reset' returns to the sample-ready state.
pedagogical.assessment: Evaluation with staged feedback: the student screens each sample, reads the presumptive verdict, then commits to a confirmatory test that either validates or overturns the screen. Meeting the horseradish false positive forces the student to articulate why a presumptive positive means 'consistent with blood, confirm it' rather than 'this is blood.'
pedagogical.modelType: Qualitative peroxidase model: each sample carries a presumptive reaction strength (0 to 1) that drives a color lerp from the sample color toward pink, plus an independent confirmatory truth flag for whether the stain is actually blood. Blood simulant and Stain A are true positives, horseradish is a strong false positive, rust reacts weakly, and ketchup is negative.
controls: 'Sample' dropdown — Stain A (reddish-brown), Rust water, Ketchup, Horseradish extract, or Blood simulant
'Run Presumptive Test' button — animate the reagent drop and develop the presumptive color
'Run Confirmatory Test' button — reveal whether the stain is truly blood
'Reset' button — return the well to the sample-ready state
